title: README documents npm commands despite pnpm package manager

evidence.quote: The commands table uses npm install and npm run commands.

reasoning: The package manifest explicitly pins pnpm, but the package context tells contributors to install and run the project with npm. Following the README can create an npm lockfile, ignore pnpm-specific lock resolution, and make local or CI dependency behavior diverge from the intended package manager.
reproduction: Follow README.md: run `npm install` and `npm run build` in a fresh checkout instead of using the pinned `pnpm` workflow.
recommendation: Update README commands to use `pnpm install`, `pnpm dev`, `pnpm build`, `pnpm preview`, and `pnpm astro ...`, or remove the starter README if it is no longer authoritative.
whyTestsDoNotAlreadyCoverThis: No tests or documentation checks are linked for this feature, and the package scripts do not validate README command accuracy.
minimumFixScope: README.md command table and any setup snippets that mention npm.
